features.featureTitle: Audit and rewrite `frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts` (without modifying the immutable `frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts` path — instead patch the internal logic exposed to consumers) so that `isActorReady` is only set to `true` after both (a) the Internet Identity principal is fully resolved and non-anonymous, and (b) the actor instance is confirmed non-null. Expose `isActorReady` as a stable boolean via the hook's return value. If the file is immutable, create a thin `useActorReady.ts` wrapper hook in `frontend/src/hooks/` that derives and exports `isActorReady` from the existing `useActor` hook.

Rewrite `frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts` — specifically the `useGetProfile` query — so that: (1) the query's `enabled` flag is strictly gated on `isActorReady === true`; (2) the React Query cache key includes the authenticated principal string (e.g. `['profile', principalText]`) so it auto-invalidates when the principal changes; (3) a `null` / empty optional returned by the backend is treated as 'profile not found' (new user flow) and not as an error; (4) only a thrown exception is treated as a genuine fetch failure. All other query hooks in the file must also gate their `enabled` flag on `isActorReady`.

Rewrite `frontend/src/components/ProtectedRoute.tsx` to implement the following strict state machine: (1) While `isActorReady` is `false` OR the profile query `isLoading` is `true` → render a full-page military-themed loading skeleton/spinner (no redirect, no children). (2) If the profile query `isError` is `true` → render a full-page error state with a gold-accented 'Retry' button that calls `refetch()`. (3) If the user is definitively unauthenticated (identity hook confirms not logged in AND actor initialization is complete) → redirect to `/login`. (4) If `data` is `null` (profile not found, new user) → render `<ProfileSetupModal>` without redirecting. (5) If profile data is present → render `<Outlet />` / children. Eliminate any race condition where the component redirects to login during the loading phase.

Audit `frontend/src/App.tsx` — specifically the `AuthGuard` component — to ensure it does not redirect unauthenticated or 'still-initializing' users to `/login` before the Internet Identity `isInitializing` / `isLoading` flag has settled to `false`. The guard must wait for initialization to complete before making any routing decision. If `isInitializing` is `true`, render the same full-page loading skeleton used in `ProtectedRoute`.
